jobs.title: Branch Executive Director
jobs.description:
Join the YMCA of Metro Atlanta Association as the Branch Executive Director for the JM Tull Gwinnett YMCA!

Job Description

The YMCA of Metro Atlanta is seeking a passionate and forward-thinking executive director with a proven, successful track record of developing a strong branch board, increasing annual giving, engaging community through increasing membership and programs while advancing the Y’s impact in the community.

This highly motivated leader who is impact-focused and entrepreneurial by nature will report directly to the Group Vice President and will have primary responsibility for day-to-day operations. Experience in and a demonstrated track record of the areas of staff supervision, membership and program development, fiscal management, fundraising, volunteer cultivation, and community engagement are required. The continued development and coaching of an experienced staff team to new levels of service will be key to achieving full potential.

Qualifications

REQUIREMENTS:

Bachelor's Degree and 8+ years overall leadership experience or high school diploma and 12+ years of directly relevant experience with the Y or closely related non-profit
2+ years of successful experience managing large teams in an executive level/senior management position
Business management skills and demonstrated ability to lead people
Proven ability to establish and maintain collaborations with community organization, corporations, and government agencies
Strong attention to detail
Passionate commitment to the YMCA mission, values, and purpose
CPR and First Aid certifications required within 30 days of hire

Preferred Requirements

Bachelor's degree in human services, social services, business or equivalent
Experience in directing total operations through volunteer development, supervision of staff, development and monitoring of branch budget, marketing and public relations, and program development
Hands-on experience working with a diverse population in a YMCA strongly preferred
12+ years of directly relevant experience with the YMCA or closely related non-profit
4+ years of management experience, strategic vision and relationship building preferably in a YMCA or other nonprofit agency

Essential Functions

Establish a culture of quality and service to meet and exceed the goals and objectives set forth in the strategic plan and standards established by the Board of Directors.
Represents and promotes the Y in the local community by developing strategic, positive working and collaborative relationships with other organizations, businesses, and governmental entities.
Develop, manage, and monitor a balanced operating budget to meet or exceed budgeted targets.
Develop and direct high-quality, relationship-based member engagement strategies.
Provide metrics to measure program/membership impact, staff, and program and membership satisfaction.
Maintain branch and association certifications in CPR, Multimedia First Aid, Blood-borne Pathogens, Metro Atlanta YMCA new staff orientation, branch orientation and branch emergency procedures. Manage delivery of related programs to exceed YMCA standards and program objectives.
Participate in branch and Association-wide activities and initiatives.
Act as the LEAD campaigner for Why It Matters Annual Giving Campaign including but not limited to volunteer recruitment, training and support, and lead as an integral member of the management team committed to reaching stated fund-raising goals.
Serve on the Executive Cabinet, liaison with appropriate professional groups in the community and serve on Metro Task Forces.
Effective board development including recruitment of board members who represent the branch community and developing a strong board committee structure.
Ensures revenue growth through a climate of innovation to develop member-focused programs that support the Y mission, goals, and strategies.
Effectively mentor, supervise, and develop branch staff and volunteers and build an exceptional culture to motivate them to achieve their personal and professional goals.
Responsible for facility management and maintenance to ensure high-quality facilities, grounds, and equipment. A culture of risk management and safety is maintained at the branch. Maintains required safety and training certifications, Emergency Action Plans, SDS Manuals, etc.

Show more

Show less

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Non-profit Organizations

jobs.title:
Program Director,
Full-Time
jobs.description:
You will be leading a culture of wellness and girl’s empowerment!

Our girls thrive among the positive, culturally-reflective female role models they meet through Girls to Women. With an approach which sees the girls' unique attributes and 'herstories' as cues to help us best serve them and their families.

You will be leading a culture of girls education!

Our program include programming around cultural awareness, STEM, videography, science, story-telling, ecology, art, reading, health, crafts, computer projects, community events, and field trips.

Who you are!

You are an experienced, trustworthy, educator who is driven to creating a safe, active, inspiring, and joyful culture for our participants, staff, and families.
You are comfortable being responsible for the overall management of our all-girl program, including staff-hiring and scheduling, spearheading program planning, coordinating field trips and consultants, keeping our spaces safe, and our girls and families happy.

Show more

Show less

jobs.title: Ascent Managing Director Client Advisor
jobs.description:
At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions and enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive at every stage of your career. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One.

Job Description

Serves as generalist relationship manager and primary point of contact for a group of moderately to highly complex client relationships. Drives and coordinates the activity of the Ascent team providing a full complement of services to the client group. Demonstrates an ability to identify, develop and close sales opportunities from both new outside clients as well as from deepening relationships with the existing clients. Expands Ascent's presence in the ultra high net worth market including representation of the business segment in the region at community events as well as within the broader bank.

Basic Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting or other related field
15 or more years of financial services experience
10 or more years of experience with ultra high net worth clients
MBA, JD, CFP, CFA, or CPA

Preferred Skills/Experience

Well-developed leadership abilities and strategic management skills
Thorough knowledge of division and relation to industry
Strong verbal and written communication skills
Well-developed analytical, decision-making and problem-solving skills
Considerable tact, diplomacy and people skills

Total compensation for this role is highly competitive and substantially above market including a generous short-term and long-term incentive bonus; base salary may differ from the range indicated in the job posting.

If there’s anything we can do to accommodate a disability during any portion of the application or hiring process, please refer to our disability accommodations for applicants.

Benefits:

Our approach to benefits and total rewards considers our team members’ whole selves and what may be needed to thrive in and outside work. That's why our benefits are designed to help you and your family boost your health, protect your financial security and give you peace of mind. Our benefits include the following:

Healthcare (medical, dental, vision)
Basic term and optional term life insurance
Short-term and long-term disability
Pregnancy disability and parental leave
401(k) and employer-funded retirement plan
Paid vacation (from two to five weeks depending on salary grade and tenure)
Up to 11 paid holiday opportunities
Adoption assistance
Sick and Safe Leave accruals of one hour for every 30 worked, up to 80 hours per calendar year unless otherwise provided by law

Review our full benefits available by employment status here.

U.S. Bank is an equal opportunity employer. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, religion, color, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or veteran status, and other factors protected under applicable law.

E-Verify

U.S. Bank participates in the U.S. Department of Homeland Security E-Verify program in all facilities located in the United States and certain U.S. territories. The E-Verify program is an Internet-based employment eligibility verification system operated by the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services. Learn more about the E-Verify program.

The salary range reflects figures based on the primary location, which is listed first. The actual range for the role may differ based on the location of the role. In addition to salary, U.S. Bank offers a comprehensive benefits package, including incentive and recognition programs, equity stock purchase 401(k) contribution and pension (all benefits are subject to eligibility requirements). Pay Range: $197,100.00 - 240,900.00

U.S. Bank will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment. U.S. Bank conducts background checks consistent with applicable local laws, including the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance and the California Fair Chance Act as well as the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance. U.S. Bank is subject to, and conducts background checks consistent with the requirements of Section 19 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA). In addition, certain positions may also be subject to the requirements of FINRA, NMLS registration, Reg Z, Reg G, OFAC, the NFA, the FCPA, the Bank Secrecy Act, the SAFE Act, and/or federal guidelines applicable to an agreement, such as those related to ethics, safety, or operational procedures.

Applicants must be able to comply with U.S. Bank policies and procedures including the Code of Ethics and Business Conduct and related workplace conduct and safety policies.

Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants.

Show more

Show less

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
Chief Operating Officer – Senior Living (New England)

Ascentria Care Alliance

$275K – $310K Base + Executive Impact

Multi-State Leadership | Growth-Focused Platform

This Is Not a Maintenance Role. This Is a Build-and-Scale Opportunity.

At Ascentria Care Alliance
, we’re entering a defining chapter.

We are not simply operating senior living communities, we are expanding, strengthening, and reimagining what high-quality, mission-driven care can look like across New England
.

We are seeking a Chief Operating Officer
who knows how to:

See around corners
Move with urgency
Build systems that scale
And most importantly—turn strategy into measurable outcomes

Why This Role Is Different

This is a true enterprise leadership role
, not a divisional caretaker.

You Will Partner Directly With The CEO To

Shape and execute growth strategy
Drive acquisitions and integration
Elevate operational performance across a multi-state footprint
Build leadership capability at scale

If you’ve ever wanted a seat at the table where real decisions get made and executed—this is it.

What You Will Own

Multi-site oversight of Skilled Nursing, Senior Living, and Post-Acute operations
Identification, evaluation, and execution of acquisitions and strategic partnerships
Scaling operational excellence, quality outcomes, and CMS performance
Strengthening census, financial performance, and workforce stability
Building and coaching high-performing Administrator and Executive Director teams
Driving accountability, consistency, and execution across all communities

Who Thrives Here

This role is built for a true operator
—not a maintainer.

You Are

A builder who has scaled systems, not just managed them
A driver of results who understands both numbers and people
Comfortable in complex, multi-site environments
Experienced in growth, acquisitions, and integration
A leader who raises the bar across teams—not just holds it

This Will Feel Like Home If You…

Currently lead multi-site senior living or SNF operations
Have successfully improved performance across multiple communities
Have been part of acquiring, integrating, or transforming healthcare organizations
Want to move beyond execution into true strategic leadership
Are energized by building something bigger than what exists today

Your Background

Senior leadership experience in Skilled Nursing, Senior Living, Hospice, or Post-Acute Care
Proven success in multi-site operational leadership
Strong track record in:
Financial performance improvement
Workforce stabilization
Quality and regulatory outcomes
Experience with growth strategy, M&A, or integration strongly preferred
Deep understanding of CMS, compliance, and healthcare operations

Why Ascentria

Ascentria Care Alliance Is a Mission-driven Nonprofit Where

Growth is not just financial—it’s impact-driven
Leadership is not just positional—it’s purposeful
And every decision ultimately connects back to people, dignity, and community

Let’s Connect

If this feels like it has your name written all over it—or even if you’re simply curious—

Let’s have a confidential conversation
.

The best operators aren’t always looking.

But they know when something worth building
shows up.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Management and Manufacturing

Industries

Internet Publishing

new_jobs.title: Branch Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
Join the YMCA of Metro Atlanta Association as the Branch Executive Director for the JM Tull Gwinnett YMCA!

Job Description

The YMCA of Metro Atlanta is seeking a passionate and forward-thinking executive director with a proven, successful track record of developing a strong branch board, increasing annual giving, engaging community through increasing membership and programs while advancing the Y’s impact in the community.

This highly motivated leader who is impact-focused and entrepreneurial by nature will report directly to the Group Vice President and will have primary responsibility for day-to-day operations. Experience in and a demonstrated track record of the areas of staff supervision, membership and program development, fiscal management, fundraising, volunteer cultivation, and community engagement are required. The continued development and coaching of an experienced staff team to new levels of service will be key to achieving full potential.

Qualifications

REQUIREMENTS:

Bachelor's Degree and 8+ years overall leadership experience or high school diploma and 12+ years of directly relevant experience with the Y or closely related non-profit
2+ years of successful experience managing large teams in an executive level/senior management position
Business management skills and demonstrated ability to lead people
Proven ability to establish and maintain collaborations with community organization, corporations, and government agencies
Strong attention to detail
Passionate commitment to the YMCA mission, values, and purpose
CPR and First Aid certifications required within 30 days of hire

Preferred Requirements

Bachelor's degree in human services, social services, business or equivalent
Experience in directing total operations through volunteer development, supervision of staff, development and monitoring of branch budget, marketing and public relations, and program development
Hands-on experience working with a diverse population in a YMCA strongly preferred
12+ years of directly relevant experience with the YMCA or closely related non-profit
4+ years of management experience, strategic vision and relationship building preferably in a YMCA or other nonprofit agency

Essential Functions

Establish a culture of quality and service to meet and exceed the goals and objectives set forth in the strategic plan and standards established by the Board of Directors.
Represents and promotes the Y in the local community by developing strategic, positive working and collaborative relationships with other organizations, businesses, and governmental entities.
Develop, manage, and monitor a balanced operating budget to meet or exceed budgeted targets.
Develop and direct high-quality, relationship-based member engagement strategies.
Provide metrics to measure program/membership impact, staff, and program and membership satisfaction.
Maintain branch and association certifications in CPR, Multimedia First Aid, Blood-borne Pathogens, Metro Atlanta YMCA new staff orientation, branch orientation and branch emergency procedures. Manage delivery of related programs to exceed YMCA standards and program objectives.
Participate in branch and Association-wide activities and initiatives.
Act as the LEAD campaigner for Why It Matters Annual Giving Campaign including but not limited to volunteer recruitment, training and support, and lead as an integral member of the management team committed to reaching stated fund-raising goals.
Serve on the Executive Cabinet, liaison with appropriate professional groups in the community and serve on Metro Task Forces.
Effective board development including recruitment of board members who represent the branch community and developing a strong board committee structure.
Ensures revenue growth through a climate of innovation to develop member-focused programs that support the Y mission, goals, and strategies.
Effectively mentor, supervise, and develop branch staff and volunteers and build an exceptional culture to motivate them to achieve their personal and professional goals.
Responsible for facility management and maintenance to ensure high-quality facilities, grounds, and equipment. A culture of risk management and safety is maintained at the branch. Maintains required safety and training certifications, Emergency Action Plans, SDS Manuals, etc.

Show more

Show less

new_jobs.title:
Program Director,
Full-Time
new_jobs.description:
You will be leading a culture of wellness and girl’s empowerment!

Our girls thrive among the positive, culturally-reflective female role models they meet through Girls to Women. With an approach which sees the girls' unique attributes and 'herstories' as cues to help us best serve them and their families.

You will be leading a culture of girls education!

Our program include programming around cultural awareness, STEM, videography, science, story-telling, ecology, art, reading, health, crafts, computer projects, community events, and field trips.

Who you are!

You are an experienced, trustworthy, educator who is driven to creating a safe, active, inspiring, and joyful culture for our participants, staff, and families.
You are comfortable being responsible for the overall management of our all-girl program, including staff-hiring and scheduling, spearheading program planning, coordinating field trips and consultants, keeping our spaces safe, and our girls and families happy.

new_jobs.title: Ascent Managing Director Client Advisor
new_jobs.description:
At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions and enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive at every stage of your career. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One.

Job Description

Serves as generalist relationship manager and primary point of contact for a group of moderately to highly complex client relationships. Drives and coordinates the activity of the Ascent team providing a full complement of services to the client group. Demonstrates an ability to identify, develop and close sales opportunities from both new outside clients as well as from deepening relationships with the existing clients. Expands Ascent's presence in the ultra high net worth market including representation of the business segment in the region at community events as well as within the broader bank.

Basic Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting or other related field
15 or more years of financial services experience
10 or more years of experience with ultra high net worth clients
MBA, JD, CFP, CFA, or CPA

Preferred Skills/Experience

Well-developed leadership abilities and strategic management skills
Thorough knowledge of division and relation to industry
Strong verbal and written communication skills
Well-developed analytical, decision-making and problem-solving skills
Considerable tact, diplomacy and people skills

Total compensation for this role is highly competitive and substantially above market including a generous short-term and long-term incentive bonus; base salary may differ from the range indicated in the job posting.

If there’s anything we can do to accommodate a disability during any portion of the application or hiring process, please refer to our disability accommodations for applicants.

Benefits:

Our approach to benefits and total rewards considers our team members’ whole selves and what may be needed to thrive in and outside work. That's why our benefits are designed to help you and your family boost your health, protect your financial security and give you peace of mind. Our benefits include the following:

Healthcare (medical, dental, vision)
Basic term and optional term life insurance
Short-term and long-term disability
Pregnancy disability and parental leave
401(k) and employer-funded retirement plan
Paid vacation (from two to five weeks depending on salary grade and tenure)
Up to 11 paid holiday opportunities
Adoption assistance
Sick and Safe Leave accruals of one hour for every 30 worked, up to 80 hours per calendar year unless otherwise provided by law

Review our full benefits available by employment status here.

U.S. Bank is an equal opportunity employer. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, religion, color, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or veteran status, and other factors protected under applicable law.

E-Verify

U.S. Bank participates in the U.S. Department of Homeland Security E-Verify program in all facilities located in the United States and certain U.S. territories. The E-Verify program is an Internet-based employment eligibility verification system operated by the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services. Learn more about the E-Verify program.

The salary range reflects figures based on the primary location, which is listed first. The actual range for the role may differ based on the location of the role. In addition to salary, U.S. Bank offers a comprehensive benefits package, including incentive and recognition programs, equity stock purchase 401(k) contribution and pension (all benefits are subject to eligibility requirements). Pay Range: $197,100.00 - 240,900.00

U.S. Bank will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment. U.S. Bank conducts background checks consistent with applicable local laws, including the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance and the California Fair Chance Act as well as the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance. U.S. Bank is subject to, and conducts background checks consistent with the requirements of Section 19 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA). In addition, certain positions may also be subject to the requirements of FINRA, NMLS registration, Reg Z, Reg G, OFAC, the NFA, the FCPA, the Bank Secrecy Act, the SAFE Act, and/or federal guidelines applicable to an agreement, such as those related to ethics, safety, or operational procedures.

Applicants must be able to comply with U.S. Bank policies and procedures including the Code of Ethics and Business Conduct and related workplace conduct and safety policies.

Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants.

new_jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
new_jobs.description:
Chief Operating Officer – Senior Living (New England)

Ascentria Care Alliance

$275K – $310K Base + Executive Impact

Multi-State Leadership | Growth-Focused Platform

This Is Not a Maintenance Role. This Is a Build-and-Scale Opportunity.

At Ascentria Care Alliance
, we’re entering a defining chapter.

We are not simply operating senior living communities, we are expanding, strengthening, and reimagining what high-quality, mission-driven care can look like across New England
.

We are seeking a Chief Operating Officer
who knows how to:

See around corners
Move with urgency
Build systems that scale
And most importantly—turn strategy into measurable outcomes

Why This Role Is Different

This is a true enterprise leadership role
, not a divisional caretaker.

You Will Partner Directly With The CEO To

Shape and execute growth strategy
Drive acquisitions and integration
Elevate operational performance across a multi-state footprint
Build leadership capability at scale

If you’ve ever wanted a seat at the table where real decisions get made and executed—this is it.

What You Will Own

Multi-site oversight of Skilled Nursing, Senior Living, and Post-Acute operations
Identification, evaluation, and execution of acquisitions and strategic partnerships
Scaling operational excellence, quality outcomes, and CMS performance
Strengthening census, financial performance, and workforce stability
Building and coaching high-performing Administrator and Executive Director teams
Driving accountability, consistency, and execution across all communities

Who Thrives Here

This role is built for a true operator
—not a maintainer.

You Are

A builder who has scaled systems, not just managed them
A driver of results who understands both numbers and people
Comfortable in complex, multi-site environments
Experienced in growth, acquisitions, and integration
A leader who raises the bar across teams—not just holds it

This Will Feel Like Home If You…

Currently lead multi-site senior living or SNF operations
Have successfully improved performance across multiple communities
Have been part of acquiring, integrating, or transforming healthcare organizations
Want to move beyond execution into true strategic leadership
Are energized by building something bigger than what exists today

Your Background

Senior leadership experience in Skilled Nursing, Senior Living, Hospice, or Post-Acute Care
Proven success in multi-site operational leadership
Strong track record in:
Financial performance improvement
Workforce stabilization
Quality and regulatory outcomes
Experience with growth strategy, M&A, or integration strongly preferred
Deep understanding of CMS, compliance, and healthcare operations

Why Ascentria

Ascentria Care Alliance Is a Mission-driven Nonprofit Where

Growth is not just financial—it’s impact-driven
Leadership is not just positional—it’s purposeful
And every decision ultimately connects back to people, dignity, and community

Let’s Connect

If this feels like it has your name written all over it—or even if you’re simply curious—

Let’s have a confidential conversation
.

The best operators aren’t always looking.

But they know when something worth building
shows up.

Show more

Show less
